decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize the current synthetic writer-swap experiment as positive; if continuing, validate the compressed writer on real coding-agent memory traces as a separate target.
decision.stop_reason: The compressed-scene dual-trace writer achieved a 41.2-point mean accuracy gain over fact-only at 5.5% mean storage overhead across 10 seeds, satisfying the stated success criteria.

recommended_next_action: Finalize the current synthetic writer-swap experiment as positive; if continuing, validate the compressed writer on real coding-agent memory traces as a separate target.
run_notes_tail: Single seed (`343`): dual trace reached 77.7% accuracy vs 29.1% for fact-only and 29.1% for volume-matched fact-only, a +48.6 point gain. Multi-seed (`340..349`) mean gain was +48.4 points with 2.7 point stdev. This supports the scene-anchor mechanism and shows the gain is not from memory volume alone.

The current writer does **not** satisfy the storage constraint: dual trace used +108.8% mean storage overhead vs fact-only, far above the ≤10% success criterion. Evidence is mixed rather than positive: contextual temporal recall improves strongly, but compactness is unresolved.

Keep this project active for one more concrete iteration: implement a compressed-scene writer constrained to ≤10% overhead, rerun the same benchmark, and finalize negative if the 15-point accuracy lift disappears under that storage budget.
1. Add a `compressed_dual_trace` writer that preserves the same event facts plus query-facing cue anchors while removing duplicate scene fields and shortening source to a one-character code.
2. Keep the retriever and answer extraction unchanged; only add the new writer condition and metrics that compare it to the existing fact-only baseline.
3. Rerun the single-seed and 10-seed benchmark, then update reports and the decision artifact based on the ≤10% storage constraint and ≥15-point gain criterion.

Implemented `compressed_dual_trace` in `scripts/dual_trace_memory_benchmark.py`. The writer keeps the fact fields and adds a single scene cue anchor while removing duplicated scene fields and coding source to one character, so retrieval/search and answer extraction remain unchanged.
Single seed (`343`): compressed dual trace reached 68.2% accuracy vs 29.1% for fact-only and 29.1% for volume-matched fact-only, a +39.1 point gain at +5.4% storage overhead.

10 seeds (`340..349`): mean compressed dual minus fact-only gain was +41.2 points (3.1 stdev) at +5.5% mean storage overhead (0.2 stdev). This satisfies both success thresholds: ≥15-point gain and ≤10% storage overhead.

The minimum viable synthetic writer-swap experiment is positive. Remaining risk is external validity: the harness is deterministic and synthetic, so the next scientifically distinct validation would be real coding-agent memory traces rather than another same-mechanism branch.
